content: Hey, I'm thinking about visiting my family in New York for Thanksgiving this year. I haven't seen them since Christmas last year.
That sounds like a great idea! How many people are you planning to visit?
Just my parents and my younger sister, Emily. She's 19 and in college now. I'm really proud of her for pursuing a degree in computer science.
That's wonderful! It's great that you're close to your family. What do you like to do when you're not working as a software engineer?
I'm really into chess and baking sourdough bread. I find it really relaxing. I've even started a small garden in my apartment in San Diego.
Those are great hobbies! Do you have any favorite chess openings or bread recipes you like to try out?
Yeah, I'm a big fan of the Ruy Lopez opening. And for bread, I love making a classic sourdough recipe with a 24-hour fermentation time. It's a lot of work, but it's worth it.
That sounds delicious! I'll have to look up that recipe. What do you think you'll do for your sister's birthday, which is coming up soon, right?
Actually, her birthday is on October 12th. I was thinking of getting her a new laptop for school. She's been using an old one for a while now.
That's very thoughtful of you. I'm sure she'll appreciate the new laptop. Do you have any other siblings or is it just you and Emily?
It's just the two of us. We're pretty close, despite the age difference. I'm looking forward to spending some quality time with her and my parents during Thanksgiving.
I'm sure you'll have a great time. If you need any more recommendations for things to do in New York, feel free to ask!

Thanks, I appreciate that. I'll definitely reach out if I need any more advice. Oh, and I almost forgot - I'm planning to take my archery gear with me to New York, so I can practice a bit while I'm there.
That's great that you're passionate about archery! Make sure to check the regulations and find a safe place to practice in New York.
Will do, thanks for the reminder. I've been practicing for about 5 years now, and I'm getting pretty good at it.
That's impressive! It's great that you've found a hobby that you enjoy and are committed to improving at.
